formatRule: PNG only. SVG, PDF and WebP are prohibited anywhere in the repo.
artDirection: Original hand-painted 2.5D travel mystery. Hamsa Nomads palette: cream/ivory #F5EDDC, deep teal #153F46, navy #182842, terracotta #C46A4A, olive #7A7C4E, sand #DEC69C, muted gold #C6A052. Paper layers, passport stamps, route lines, subtle parallax. Leah: Jewish woman, tsniut, headscarf, long sleeves, long skirt. Moshé: slim, curly dark hair, short beard, visible kippah. Never redesign them between countries. Never copy Professor Layton art/UI.
note: Every file below currently exists as a flat-colour placeholder written by tools/generate_placeholders.py. Replace each with painted art at the suggested size, keeping the exact path. Files marked keep:true are existing hand-made assets and must not be overwritten by the placeholder script.

The Restless Invitation: Letters moved when the invitation changed. They settle into a hidden instruction.
One Suitcase, Two Nomads: Pack without crushing the wedding gift or burying the passports.
The Flight That Still Exists: Only one flight leaves enough time for the Muscat connection.
The Broken Baggage Scale: Use exactly three reference weights to balance the 22 kg suitcase.
Four Cities, Four Clocks: Set every clock to the same instant as 09:00 in Paris.
The Gate in the Tiles: Leah feels the mechanical seams: four symbols must be pressed in order.
The Vanishing Suitcase: Who accidentally took the green Mumbai suitcase?
Muscat Under the Stars: Moshé reads the route diagram; Leah turns the star ceiling. Neither works alone.
The Seating Problem: Seven passengers, seven seats, and the gate is closing.
Two Corridors, One Arrival: Leah sees the power controls; Moshé sees the doors. Each control opens the other's path.
The Havdalah Order: Set the Havdalah items in the order they are used.
The Object from Later: One object on the debris hill does not belong to this afternoon.
Footprints That Never Left: Read the beach: whose prints are whose, and what is impossible about them?
Two Skylines: Leah sees structures; Moshé sees the city's story. Only overlaid do they match the view.
The Opinionated Bridge: Cross with camera, dinner and scooter — the rotten plank takes at most one light load.
The Settlement Model: Leah places structures; Moshé arranges the plan. The model wants both.
Fourteen of Sixteen: Sixteen volunteers, fourteen in the photo. Who is missing?
Four Quarters, One Path: Cross the Old City visiting each quarter once, never crossing your own path.
Six Wrong Turns: Recount the six wrong turns of that Shabbat walk. Drawn on paper, they make a letter.
The Sentence in the Signs: Moshé reads the street signs in walking order. They form a question.
One Battery, Two Bikes: 40% battery, two bikes, 8 km of gravel. Assist drains 10% per km but only one bike can use it at a time.
The Shuffled Shabbat: The border day refuses to stay in order. Rebuild it.
The Pouch of Old Tickets: Reconstruct the Riviera hops from the ticket stubs — and notice what every stub shares.
Six of Seven: The booklet holds six blessings. Which of the seven is missing?
The Stone Doubles: Leah maps the niches; Moshé matches the faces. Cross-check both lists.
The Arguing Timetable: Board says platform 5, sign says 3, conductor says yes. The Udaipur train leaves in 9 minutes.
The Cable Car Limit: The car takes 200 kg per trip. Loads: 90, 80, 70, 60, 50. Everyone rides before the fireworks — in three trips.
The Wall's Rhythm: The Amber wall repeats every five merlons, then breaks. Breaks mark the path.
The Case of Fuzzy's Tail: Fuzzy the costume-dog has lost his tail during Purim-meets-Holi. Everyone is pink.
The Fair Meter: Quoted 300. The meter says 8 per km, the map says 12 km, and dignity is non-negotiable.
The Flaw in the Reflection: Building and reflection differ in exactly one detail.
Camp Before Dark: Two workers, four jobs, one correct interleaving before the light goes.
Four Stops Before Closing: Chabad, Chinatown, the floating market and the pier — one boat line, one direction, market closes first.
The Sea's Timetable: The lagoon needs mid-tide; the cave mouth needs anything below high.
The Load-Bearing Coconuts: Seven coconuts, three smoothie orders: one double, two singles. A double takes two coconuts... and Moshé needs two just to hold.
Navigation by Pictures: No shared language. The guesthouse card shows: fish, umbrella, golden bowl, moon.
Seder by the Sea: The outdoor seder must begin in order — before the water festival reaches the table.
The Elephant's Round: The elephant visited: pagoda, bridge, pagoda, gate, pagoda, bridge, pagoda, ... The bananas move in a pattern.
Road of Frost and Haze: High road closes at noon. Hot spring adds an hour. Temple face lights at four.
The Broken Run: Flags run: three red, one banner, repeat. One balcony breaks the run.
Rows of Two: Six riders, three rows. Birthday girl picks front. The tall friend can't ride behind the hat. The couple won't split.
Lanterns and Reflections: Leah reads the river reflections; Moshé reads the lanterns. Light them so both patterns agree.
The Spinning Basket: The basket boat spins unless the weight sits opposite and low.
Gate Within Gate: Outer gate: east door for travelers. Middle gate: scholars pass south. Inner gate: the hinge turns one way.
Three Cave Mouths: Left cave: under a meter clearance. Right cave: dry ledge, no water. Middle cave: sings.
The Terrace Rhythm: The terraces descend 4, 4, 4, 3, 4, 4, 4, 3 … until one group breaks the rhythm.
The Extraction: Bike in the mud to the axle. Two people, four moves, one order that works.
The Echo Chamber: The cave returns each word altered toward its truth. You called GO. What did the cave answer?
Signs and Their Twins: Six reflections in the canal, five signs above. Pair what pairs, and name the orphan.
The Queue Theorem: Left lane: merges every 10 min. Right lane: slow but never stops. Express gate: opens on the quarter hour, from the right lane only.
The Hamsa at the Gates: Hold the Hamsa to each gate. Leah reads the eye; Moshé reads the inscriptions. True gates keep the eye clear.
The Map Thief of Nara: Three deer came within reach: one bowed twice (both hands visible — no maps), one has paper crumbs, one arrived after the bag was already light.
Two Maps of One City: Leah's layer: rail load and flow. Moshé's layer: districts and eras. One crossing satisfies both.
The Day in Photographs: Order the day's photos by light and logic — then look at what every gate photo shares.
The Compass Gates: The palace gates open by compass: begin where the sun rises, end where it sets, honoring the main gate between.
You Are My…: He has been building the sentence all day. The last word is a city wearing a word inside it.
The Page, Rebuilt: Eight fragments, one erased page. Order them by the journey.
People Don't Keep Receipts for Jokes: Find the object that proves the thrift-shop wedding rehearsal meant something.
The Beach, From Her Side: Replay the sunset from Leah's viewpoint. What could she see that he couldn't?
Six Postcards, Two Truths: Test each proposal place against the Hamsa's eye. Four cloud it; two keep it clear.
Finale I — The Order of Things: Lay the whole story straight: the four turning points, in the order they truly happened.
Finale II — The True Memories: Three of these are true and load-bearing. One is an invention the reconstruction slipped in.
Finale III — The Last Line: The boarding pass is whole except its final line. It is not a destination. It is what Moshé said instead of boarding the missing flight — three words.

assets.note: Existing hand-made 7x7 sprite atlases, frame 24x38 px logical grid. Rows: idle, walk, talk, question, jump, interact, extra. Replace later with full state atlases (idle/walk in 4 directions, listen, think, point, examine, pickup, celebrate, surprised, worried, laugh, tired, wet, bicycle, motorbike, sitting, map, camera, passport, coop action) and document frame sizes here.
